strengths: Three authentic, specific real-world objects are clearly identified and situated in context (textbook, Siri screenshot, Disney still).
Explicit, generally convincing links are made between each object and the IA prompt (âIs bias inevitable in the production of knowledge?â).
Concrete illustrative details (regional textbook interpretations, non-consensual kiss example) anchor the analysis and move beyond mere description.
Frequent reference to the prompt throughout and a synthesis paragraph that ties all objects back to the overarching claim of pervasive bias.
Selection of objects demonstrates topical range (educational text, technology, cultural media), showcasing bias in diverse knowledge contexts.
weaknesses: Key assertions (state intent, Appleâs motives, Disneyâs universal influence) lack robust supporting evidence or citation of external sources.
TOK terminology (ways of knowing, areas of knowledge, counterclaims) is only implicit or absent, reducing theoretical depth.
Analysis of counterclaims and mitigation strategies for bias is underdeveloped or omitted.
Precision is uneven: some object contexts (textbook publication details, user-study data for Siri) are too vague.
Conclusion sections occasionally restate points without explicitly linking back to the prompt or TOK concepts.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs exhibition meets the descriptors for band 7â8: three specific objects are contextualized, links to the prompt are clearly explained, and most points are supported with illustrative detail. However, uneven depth of evidence, lack of explicit TOK terminology, and limited counterclaim evaluation prevent placement in the top band. A score of 7 accurately reflects strong overall performance with room to strengthen theoretical precision and evidentiary support.

annotations.comment: This analysis asserts government intent behind the textbook narrative but offers no supporting evidence or references. Incorporate curriculum documents or academic sources to justify claims about state bias.

The concluding remarks on Object 1 restate bias implications but lack explicit linkage to the prompt. Reframe to show how this example directly answers âIs bias inevitable in the production of knowledge?â.
Identifying the non-consensual kiss in âSleeping Beautyâ offers a nuanced example of gender bias, demonstrating how cultural products can perpetuate problematic narratives.
The detailed alt text description for the textbook cover is excellent for accessibility and clarity.
Consider explicitly naming the ways of knowing (e.g., memory, language) through which biases enter historical narratives to deepen the TOK analysis.
Selecting a specific Vietnamese Grade 5 textbook grounds your exhibition in a concrete real-world object, effectively meeting the criterion of authentic context.
Attributing Siriâs bias solely to its male engineers oversimplifies the development process. Consider exploring algorithmic training data and corporate design practices to deepen the analysis.

The introduction outlines bias broadly but does not reference the specific IA prompt or use TOK terminology such as ways of knowing or areas of knowledge. Explicitly linking the prompt and integrating TOK concepts would strengthen the theoretical framing.
The title clearly states the chosen prompt but adding a brief subtitle indicating the three objects could orient the reader more effectively from the outset.

The critique of Siriâs default voice relies on general assumptions about user perceptions without empirical evidence. Incorporate user studies or statistics to substantiate claims about gender bias in AI adoption.
The discussion of Appleâs corrective action lacks evaluation of its impact. Analyze user awareness data or adoption rates to assess whether the added male voice effectively mitigates bias.
The description of the âSleeping Beautyâ screenshot is thorough, ensuring readers can visualize the scene precisely.
The screenshot of Siri is a clear, specific digital artefact that concretely illustrates bias in technology, effectively anchoring your discussion in a modern context.

While the object title specifies the textbook, the description of its real-world context (e.g., publication date, publisherâs role) is vague. Add specific contextual details to meet the criterion fully.
The conclusion of Object 3 restates the inevitability of bias but could further explain how hierarchy and identity as TOK concepts interact to shape knowledge production.
The paragraph is abruptly cut off, leaving the explanation incomplete. Ensure each section fully articulates its point and transitions smoothly to maintain coherence.
After Object 1 you move directly to conclusions about inevitability. Consider presenting a counterclaim (e.g., textbooks revised through peer review) to demonstrate balanced evaluation.
The image alt text for the Siri screenshot effectively conveys the visual content, enhancing understanding for non-visual review.
Your discussion of early-life bias formation could be enriched by referencing TOK concepts such as memory and emotion as ways of knowing, linking theory to analysis.
The works cited list is comprehensive, but the essay lacks in-text citations to clearly connect evidence to your claims. Integrate parenthetical references or footnotes for clarity.
This section asserts that beauty standards are unrealistic but could be enhanced by citing feminist film theory or scholarly critiques to substantiate the claim.
The synthesis paragraph effectively ties all three objects back to the core claim, demonstrating an overarching awareness of bias themes across diverse contexts.
The claim that Western film biases have become universal via cultural diffusion is broad. Support this with cross-cultural studies or distribution data to show the filmâs reach and impact.

The note on bias as potentially beneficial is intriguing but underdeveloped. Link this idea back to practical TOK methods for evaluating and managing bias to fulfill the exhibitionâs analytical depth.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.

rubricCriterionStrands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
